title: Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Housing Related Coordinated Entry * Homeless Youth 4 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Domestic Violence Issues Homeless Families Homeless Veterans Homeless Youth Human Trafficking Issues Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BOYS & GIRLS AID BOYS & GIRLS AID BEAVERTON TRANSITIONAL LIVING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary housing and support for homeless youth and young adults. The program is designed for young people to work with staff in learning life skills for independent living. Eligibility: Ages 18-24, homeless or a history of housing instability, fleeing domestic violence, low income as defined by federal guidelines Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-5pm, Tuesday 8:30am-5pm, Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 542-2350 Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Access Center is a service for homeless and runaway youth who are seeking intake assessment and referral. Provides basic needs including food, clothing, basic medical supplies, and shelter. Youth receive an assessment to determine if they meet homeless youth services requirements. For youth in crisis, emergency shelter is provided at Porch Light Youth Shelter. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are homeless or at immediate risk of becoming homeless; eligibility is determined through an assessment with the agency Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES NEW ROADS LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to housing programs, including shelter information, other housing resources, tenant support, and non-housing resources. Does not provide emergency housing or rental assistance, and there may be a waitlist. 941 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 8:30am-4:45pm, Wednesday 8:30am-2pm and 3:30pm-4:45pm Email (541) 686-4310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF This is an access point for being placed on Jackson County's Centralized Interest List for housing. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or families with children ages 18 and younger, who are experiencing homelessnesss Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER LOOKING GLASS COMMUNITY SERVICES LANE COUNTY COORDINATED ENTRY ACCESS POINTS MASLOW PROJECT JACKSON COUNTY CONTINUUM OF CARE SCREENINGS
